```{r}
print(outlier_report)

# 6. TOP FEATURE DRIVERS — FACTOR 1 (from Full Model)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
get_top_features <- function(model, view_name, n = 10) {
  w <- get_weights(model, views = view_name, factors = 1)[[1]]
  top_df <- data.frame(
    Feature = rownames(w),
    Weight  = w[, 1]
  )
  top_df <- top_df[order(abs(top_df$Weight), decreasing = TRUE), ]
  return(head(top_df, n))
}

top_species     <- get_top_features(model_full, "species",     10)
top_metabolites <- get_top_features(model_full, "metabolites", 10)

final_table <- rbind(
  cbind(View = "Microbiome",  top_species),
  cbind(View = "Metabolites", top_metabolites)
)

final_table$Feature <- gsub("s__", "", final_table$Feature)
final_table$Feature <- gsub("\\.",  " ", final_table$Feature)

write.csv(final_table,
          "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Table1_Factor1_Drivers.csv",
          row.names = FALSE)

print(final_table)

# 7. SAVE BEST SENSITIVITY MODEL
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
saveRDS(best_model_clean,
        "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_SENSITIVITY.rds")



# 1. Manually load the best sensitivity model from your temporary folder
# Use the path shown in your 'Saving model' log line
best_model_clean <- load_model("/var/folders/k2/4rg347055jj2_b20pxbz89100000gn/T//Rtmp6Ann5R/mofa_20260324-162559.hdf5")

cat("\n--- SEARCHING FOR ROBUSTNESS MATCH (Full F1 vs Sensitivity) ---\n")

# Loop through all 3 factors in the clean model to find where F1 went
for (f_new in 1:3) {
  cat("\nChecking Sensitivity Factor", f_new, ":\n")
  for (v in c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")) {
    w_full  <- get_weights(model_full, views = v, factors = 1)[[1]]
    w_clean <- get_weights(best_model_clean, views = v, factors = f_new)[[1]]
    
    common  <- intersect(rownames(w_full), rownames(w_clean))
    r <- cor(w_full[common, 1], w_clean[common, 1], method = "spearman")
    cat("  View:", v, "| rho =", round(r, 3), "\n")
  }
}

# Check which factor in the sensitivity model is most driven by SA
cat("\n--- S. aureus Loading in Sensitivity Model ---\n")
sa_loadings <- get_weights(best_model_clean, views = "species")[[1]]
sa_row <- sa_loadings["s__Staphylococcus_aureus", ]
print(round(sa_row, 3))

# See the top 5 drivers for the factor where SA is highest
best_sa_factor <- which.max(abs(sa_row))
cat("\nTop Drivers for Sensitivity Factor", best_sa_factor, ":\n")
plot_top_weights(best_model_clean, view = "species", factor = best_sa_factor, nfeatures = 5)


# --- heatmap of to SA-associated metabolites across samples--------

# 1. PREPARE THE DATA FRAME MANUALLY
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract colData safely
metadata <- as.data.frame(colData(mae_data))

# Double check if eg_id is actually in the metadata
# If it's missing, we'll use the rownames (Sample IDs) instead
if(!"eg_id" %in% colnames(metadata)){
    # Fallback: identify outliers by searching for the ID in the Sample Name
    is_outlier <- grepl("GYECZ1187|GYECZ1197", rownames(metadata))
} else {
    is_outlier <- metadata$eg_id %in% c("GYECZ1187", "GYECZ1197")
}

# 2. CREATE THE ANNOTATION DATA FRAME
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
anno_df <- data.frame(
  Status = ifelse(is_outlier, "Outlier", "Main_Cohort"),
  row.names = rownames(metadata)
)

# 3. EXTRACT TOP METABOLITES (FACTOR 1)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
w_metab <- get_weights(model_full, views = "metabolites", factors = 1)[[1]]
top_15_metabs <- rownames(w_metab)[order(abs(w_metab[, 1]), decreasing = TRUE)][1:15]

metab_data <- assay(mae_data[["metabolites"]])[top_15_metabs, ]
metab_scaled <- t(scale(t(metab_data))) # Z-score

# 4. PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(pheatmap)
pheatmap(metab_scaled,
         main = "Factor 1 Metabolite Drivers (N=17)",
         annotation_col = anno_df,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(50),
         cluster_cols = TRUE, # This will show if outliers group together
         show_colnames = TRUE,
         border_color = NA)
```

```{r}
# 1. LOAD MODELS
model_full  <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_10_factors_FINAL.rds")
model_clean <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_SENSITIVITY.rds")


# 2. CALCULATE ROBUSTNESS (Species View)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
w_full  <- get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=1)[[1]]
w_clean <- get_weights(model_clean, views="species", factors=1)[[1]]
common  <- intersect(rownames(w_full), rownames(w_clean))

robustness_species <- cor(w_full[common, 1], w_clean[common, 1])

# 3. CALCULATE ROBUSTNESS (Metabolites View)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
w_full_met  <- get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=1)[[1]]
w_clean_met <- get_weights(model_clean, views="metabolites", factors=1)[[1]]
common_met  <- intersect(rownames(w_full_met), rownames(w_clean_met))

robustness_metab <- cor(w_full_met[common_met, 1], w_clean_met[common_met, 1])

# 4. PRINT SUMMARY REPORT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n--- PIPELINE ROBUSTNESS REPORT ---\n")
cat("Species Factor 1 Correlation:     ", round(robustness_species, 3), "\n")
cat("Metabolites Factor 1 Correlation: ", round(robustness_metab, 3), "\n")
cat("----------------------------------\n")

if(robustness_species > 0.7) {
  cat("RESULT: HIGH ROBUSTNESS. Factor 1 is a valid Cohort Trend.\n")
} else {
  cat("RESULT: LOW ROBUSTNESS. Factor 1 is driven by individual extremes.\n")
}
```

```{r}
# 1. Select Top Features for each view based on Factor 1 weights
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
get_top_feats <- function(model, view, factor, n=10) {
  w <- get_weights(model, views=view, factors=factor)[[1]]
  abs_w <- sort(abs(w[,1]), decreasing = TRUE)
  return(names(abs_w)[1:n])
}

top_sp  <- get_top_feats(model_full, "species", 1, 10)
top_met <- get_top_feats(model_full, "metabolites", 1, 10)
top_path <- get_top_feats(model_full, "pathways", 1, 10)

# 2. Extract and Combine Data
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We'll use the scale=TRUE data from the model
all_data <- list()
for(v in c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")) {
  feats <- if(v=="species") top_sp else if(v=="metabolites") top_met else top_path
  all_data[[v]] <- t(model_full@data[[v]][[1]][feats, ])
}
combined_mat <- do.call(cbind, all_data)

# 3. Final Publication Plot
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Sort samples by Factor 1 score to show the 'gradient'
f1_scores <- get_factors(model_full)$group1[,1]
combined_mat <- combined_mat[order(f1_scores), ]

png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/FINAL_Integration_Heatmap.png", 
    width = 1500, height = 900, res = 150)

pheatmap(t(combined_mat), 
         cluster_cols = FALSE, # Keep them ordered by Factor 1
         scale = "row",
         annotation_col = sample_anno, 
         annotation_colors = anno_colours,
         gaps_row = c(10, 20), # Visual gaps between Species/Metab/Path
         main = "Multi-Omic Signature of Factor 1",
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#D6604D"))(100))

dev.off()

# 1. GET ACTIVE FACTORS
# (Remember, MOFA+ may have dropped some inactive factors)
n_factors_full  <- ncol(get_factors(model_full)$group1)
n_factors_clean <- ncol(get_factors(model_clean)$group1)
n_to_check      <- min(n_factors_full, n_factors_clean)

robustness_results <- data.frame(Factor = 1:n_to_check, Species_Cor = NA, Metab_Cor = NA)

# 2. LOOP THROUGH ALL FACTORS
for (i in 1:n_to_check) {
  # Species Correlation
  w_f_sp <- get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=i)[[1]]
  w_c_sp <- get_weights(model_clean, views="species", factors=i)[[1]]
  common_sp <- intersect(rownames(w_f_sp), rownames(w_c_sp))
  robustness_results$Species_Cor[i] <- cor(w_f_sp[common_sp, 1], w_c_sp[common_sp, 1])
  
  # Metabolites Correlation
  w_f_met <- get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=i)[[1]]
  w_c_met <- get_weights(model_clean, views="metabolites", factors=i)[[1]]
  common_met <- intersect(rownames(w_f_met), rownames(w_c_met))
  robustness_results$Metab_Cor[i] <- cor(w_f_met[common_met, 1], w_c_met[common_met, 1])
}

# 3. VIEW THE MAP
print(robustness_results)

# 4. SAVE FOR SUPPLEMENTARY DATA
write.csv(robustness_results, "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Full_Robustness_Report.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

### Visualisations

```{r}
# 1. SETUP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
mofa_to_plot <- readRDS("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/mofa_model_v4_BEST.rds")

# Ensure the output directory exists
output_dir <- "~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# 2. VARIANCE EXPLAINED
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
p1 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, x="view", y="factor") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

p2 <- plot_variance_explained(mofa_to_plot, plot_total = TRUE)

ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_per_factor_v5.png"), 
       plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white")
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_variance_total_v5.png"), 
       plot = p2, width = 6, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg="white")

# 3. FACTOR SCORES HEATMAP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Extract factors for our single group
factors <- get_factors(mofa_to_plot)$group1 

# Create sample annotation based on row names
# PRO TIP: Check head(rownames(factors)) to ensure the 'NONLES' string is there
sample_anno <- data.frame(
  Status = ifelse(grepl("NONLES", rownames(factors)), "NONLES", "LES"),
  row.names = rownames(factors)
)

anno_colours <- list(Status = c(LES = "#E41A1C", NONLES = "#377EB8"))

png(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_heatmap_v5.png"), 
    width = 1300, height = 800, res = 120, bg = "white")

pheatmap(factors,
         annotation_row = sample_anno,
         annotation_colors = anno_colours,
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         scale = "column", 
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#D6604D"))(100),
         main = "MOFA+ Factor Scores per Sample",
         fontsize = 12,
         cellwidth = 50)
dev.off()

# Plot the top 15 features (microbes or pathways) for Factor 1
p_weights <- plot_top_weights(mofa_to_plot, 
                              view = "pathways", 
                              factor = 1, 
                              nfeatures = 15)
ggsave(paste0(output_dir, "mofa_top_pathways_weights_F1.png"), p_weights)

# 1. DEFINE THE FACTORS YOU WANT TO PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We check how many factors are actually in your model (likely 7 based on your output)
active_factors <- 1:ncol(get_factors(model_full)$group1)

# 2. THE MULTI-FACTOR LOOP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
for (f in active_factors) {
  
  cat("\n--- Generating Heatmap for Factor", f, "---")
  
  # A. Select top 10 features for THIS factor (f)
  top_sp   <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="species", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10]
  top_met  <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="metabolites", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10]
  top_path <- names(sort(abs(get_weights(model_full, views="pathways", factors=f)[[1]][,1]), decreasing=T))[1:10]
  
  # B. Extract Data
  list_data <- list(
    Microbiome  = model_full@data$species[[1]][top_sp, ],
    Metabolites = model_full@data$metabolites[[1]][top_met, ],
    Pathways    = model_full@data$pathways[[1]][top_path, ]
  )
  combined_mat <- do.call(rbind, list_data)
  
  # C. Re-order samples by the current factor (f) scores
  # This ensures each heatmap shows a smooth transition for THAT factor
  f_scores <- get_factors(model_full)$group1[, f]
  combined_mat <- combined_mat[, order(f_scores)]
  
  # D. Sync Annotation
  sample_anno_ordered <- sample_anno[colnames(combined_mat), , drop=FALSE]
  rownames(combined_mat) <- clean_label(rownames(combined_mat))
  
  # E. Save File
  file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/Heatmap_Factor_", f, ".png")
  
  png(file_name, width = 2000, height = 1200, res = 150)
  
  pheatmap(combined_mat, 
           cluster_cols = FALSE, 
           cluster_rows = TRUE, 
           scale = "row", 
           annotation_col = sample_anno_ordered, 
           annotation_colors = anno_colours,
           gaps_row = c(10, 20),
           main = paste("Integrated Multi-Omic Signature: Factor", f),
           color = colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(100),
           fontsize_row = 10,
           cellwidth = 30)
  
  dev.off()
}

cat("\n--- ALL HEATMAPS SAVED TO DESKTOP ---")

# Simple T-test for each factor against Status
# 1. Create a clean, dedicated Data Frame from your master_table
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We pull from master_table and ensure it's a data.frame, not a character vector
meta_to_add <- master_table %>%
  select(sample = NMR_sample_id, Status = lesional) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  as.data.frame() # Force the 'data.frame' class to avoid signature errors

# 2. Filter to match the samples actually in your model
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# This handles the sample difference (N=17 or N=15) automatically
current_samples <- samples_names(model_full)[[1]]
meta_to_add <- meta_to_add[meta_to_add$sample %in% current_samples, ]

# 3. Assign using the correct MOFA2 function
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Wrapping in as.data.frame resolves the 'signature = character' error
samples_metadata(model_full) <- as.data.frame(meta_to_add)

# 4. Final Verification
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
cat("Success! Group counts in model:\n")
print(table(model_full@samples_metadata$Status))

# 5. Re-run Significance Test for all 10 Factors
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
f_mat <- get_factors(model_full)$group1
status_vec <- as.factor(model_full@samples_metadata$Status)

sig_results <- data.frame(
  Factor = paste0("Factor ", 1:ncol(f_mat)),
  P_Value = sapply(1:ncol(f_mat), function(i) {
    # Perform t-test between LES and NONLES for each factor
    t.test(f_mat[,i] ~ status_vec)$p.value
  })
)

sig_results$Significant <- ifelse(sig_results$P_Value < 0.05, "YES", "no")
print(sig_results)


# 1. Align SA abundance from master_table to the model factors
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
factors_df <- as.data.frame(get_factors(model_full)$group1)
factors_df$sample <- rownames(factors_df)

# Link SA abundance and EASI from your master_table
clinical_data <- master_table %>%
  select(sample = NMR_sample_id, sa_abundance, EASI) %>%
  distinct()

factors_df <- factors_df %>%
  left_join(clinical_data, by = "sample")

# 2. Correlate all factors with SA abundance
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sa_correlations <- sapply(1:7, function(i) {
  cor.test(factors_df[[paste0("Factor", i)]], factors_df$sa_abundance, method = "spearman")$p.value
})

# 3. Correlate all factors with EASI
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
easi_correlations <- sapply(1:7, function(i) {
  cor.test(factors_df[[paste0("Factor", i)]], factors_df$EASI, method = "spearman")$p.value
})

# 4. Final Clinical Significance Report
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
clinical_sig <- data.frame(
  Factor = paste0("Factor ", 1:7),
  P_vs_Status = sig_results$P_Value, # From your previous run
  P_vs_SA = round(sa_correlations, 5),
  P_vs_EASI = round(easi_correlations, 5)
)

print(clinical_sig)

# 1. EXTRACT FACTOR SCORES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
f_scores <- as.data.frame(get_factors(model_full)$group1)
f_scores$sample <- rownames(f_scores)

# 2. CREATE A CLEAN MAPPING TABLE FROM MASTER_TABLE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We extract only the necessary columns and remove duplicates
clinical_map <- master_table %>%
  select(sample = NMR_sample_id, sa_abundance, Status = lesional) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  filter(!is.na(sample))

# 3. JOIN AND CLEAN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# We join the clinical data to the factor scores using the sample IDs
plot_data <- f_scores %>%
  left_join(clinical_map, by = "sample") %>%
  mutate(
    sa_abundance = as.numeric(sa_abundance),
    Factor2 = as.numeric(Factor2),
    Status = as.factor(Status)
  )

# 4. GENERATE THE SCATTER PLOT
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
library(ggplot2)

p_f2_sa <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Factor2, colour = Status)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NONLES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(
    title = "MOFA+ Factor 2 vs. S. aureus Abundance",
    subtitle = "Significant Multi-Omic Driver (p < 0.00001)",
    x = "S. aureus Abundance (%)",
    y = "Factor 2 Score",
    colour = "Status"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# 5. PRINT AND SAVE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
print(p_f2_sa)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F2_vs_SA_Scatter_MANUAL_FIX.png", 
       plot = p_f2_sa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

### 4.3. Correlate Factor Scores with S. Aureus Abundance

Correlate MOFA+ factor scores with SA abundance to identify which factor tracks with SA.

**Why do this? -\>** MOFA+ factors are hidden variables capturing a pattern of co-variation across data. When factors is correlated with SA abundance and score consistently high it means samples from SA high patients tend to score high on factor X (vice versa for low SA). Which would mean the biological variation captured by factor X tracks with SA abundance. Finding out this would help you answer the biological story of what changes in the microbiome and metabolome when SA is high.

So the Spearman correlation are between each factor's score with SA and EASI (lesionality). For each factor (1-4), across all 18 samples, asking if that samples score on that factor track with how much SA that sample has.

```{r}
# Add SA abundance to factors dataframe
factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors)
factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
]
factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
]
factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)
]

# -------- F1&2 Correlations with SA & EASI -------------
# Spearman correlations with SA - Factors 1 and 2 only
sa_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2], 
               factors_df$sa_abundance,
               method = "spearman",
               use = "complete.obs")

print(sa_cors)
# Spearman correlations with EASI - Factors 1 and 2 only
easi_cors <- cor(factors_df[, 1:2],
                 factors_df$EASI,
                 method = "spearman",
                 use = "complete.obs")

print(easi_cors)
# Run cor.test for each factor against SA and EASI
# p values for Factors 1 and 2 only
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) {
  cat("\n---", f, "---\n")
  
  sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], 
                      factors_df$sa_abundance,
                      method = "spearman", exact=FALSE)
  cat("SA:   rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3),
      "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
  
  easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], 
                        factors_df$EASI,
                        method = "spearman", exact=FALSE)
  cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3),
      "  p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
}

#------- F1&2 Correlations with all Clincal Variables-------
# Select numeric clinical variables
clinical_vars <- master_table %>%
  select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean,
         weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>%
  mutate(
    TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)),
    pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)),
    EASI = as.numeric(EASI),
    age = as.numeric(age)
  )
# Match to factor scores by NMR sample ID
clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), 
]

# Correlate all clinical variables against Factors 1 and 2
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:2)) {
  cat("\n======", f, "======\n")
  for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) {
    test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]],
                     method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
    cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3),
        "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n")
  }
}

# Add lesional status to factors_df and plot Factor 1 by lesional status
F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1, fill = lesional)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1) +
  labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") +
  theme_classic()

# Test it statistically
wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality3.png",
       plot = F1_lesional,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300) 

# test all factors correlation with lesionality
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) {
  test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional)
  cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n")
}


```

Exploring the factors

```{r}
plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 =10)


# --- Top Weights ----------
# Plots top n features with the highest absolute loading value
# for a specific factor
# Good for identifying the main drivers of a factor
# high absolute loading value >0.8

target_factors <- 1:4
target_views <- c("species", "metabolites")

for (f in target_factors) {
  for (v in target_views) {
    
    # 1. Create the Top Weights plot
    p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj, 
                          view = v, 
                          factor = f, 
                          nfeatures = 10, 
                          scale = TRUE) + 
      ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)) # Adjust font here
    
    # 2. Create a dynamic filename
    file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_", v, "_weights_F", f, ".png")
    
    # 3. Save
    ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    
    message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v))
  }
}

```

Three-view model with pathways

```{r}
# ------- Post-hoc Analysis - 3 View Model -------

# Add clinical variables to factors dataframe
factors_df <- as.data.frame(factors)
factors_df$sa_abundance <- master_table$sa_abundance[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
factors_df$EASI <- master_table$EASI[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
factors_df$lesional <- master_table$lesional[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]

# -------- Correlations with all factors (not just 1&2) -------------
# Now we know Factors 1 and 2 are SA-associated, test all 4
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) {
  cat("\n---", f, "---\n")
  
  sa_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]],
                      factors_df$sa_abundance,
                      method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  cat("SA:   rho =", round(sa_test$estimate, 3),
      "  p =", round(sa_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
  
  easi_test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]],
                        factors_df$EASI,
                        method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
  cat("EASI: rho =", round(easi_test$estimate, 3),
      "  p =", round(easi_test$p.value, 3), "\n")
}

# -------- Correlations with all clinical variables -------
clinical_vars <- master_table %>%
  select(sa_abundance, EASI, age, TEWL_mean, pH_mean,
         weeks_since_last_TCS_or_TCI) %>%
  mutate(
    TEWL_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, TEWL_mean)),
    pH_mean = as.numeric(gsub("none|not_done", NA, pH_mean)),
    EASI = as.numeric(EASI),
    age = as.numeric(age)
  )
clinical_vars <- clinical_vars[
  match(rownames(factors_df), master_table$NMR_sample_id), ]

# Test all 4 factors against all clinical variables
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) {
  cat("\n======", f, "======\n")
  for(v in colnames(clinical_vars)) {
    test <- cor.test(factors_df[[f]], clinical_vars[[v]],
                     method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
    cat(v, ": rho =", round(test$estimate, 3),
        "  p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n")
  }
}

# -------- Lesionality -------
F1_lesional <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = lesional, y = Factor1, 
                                       fill = lesional)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("LES" = "#F8766D", "NON_LES" = "#00BFC4")) +
  labs(title = "Factor 1 scores by lesional status") +
  theme_classic()

wilcox.test(Factor1 ~ lesional, data = factors_df)

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_Lesionality_3view.png",
       plot = F1_lesional, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

# Test all factors against lesionality
for(f in paste0("Factor", 1:4)) {
  test <- wilcox.test(factors_df[[f]] ~ factors_df$lesional)
  cat(f, ": p =", round(test$p.value, 3), "\n")
}

# -------- Variance explained -------
plot_variance_explained(mofa_obj, max_r2 = 10)

# -------- Top weights - now includes pathways view -------
target_factors <- 1:4
target_views <- c("species", "metabolites", "pathways")  # added pathways

for(f in target_factors) {
  for(v in target_views) {
    p <- plot_top_weights(mofa_obj,
                          view = v,
                          factor = f,
                          nfeatures = 10,
                          scale = TRUE) +
      ggtitle(paste("Top 10", v, "Weights for Factor", f)) +
      theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10))
    
    file_name <- paste0("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/top_",
                        v, "_weights_F", f, "_3view.png")
    ggsave(file_name, plot = p, width = 9, height = 6, dpi = 300)
    message(paste("Saved plot for Factor", f, "View", v))
  }
}

# Factor 1 scores by SA abundance - scatter plot
factors_df$sa_abundance <- as.numeric(factors_df$sa_abundance)

p_f1_sa <- ggplot(factors_df, aes(x = sa_abundance, y = Factor1, 
                                   colour = lesional)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, colour = "black", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("LES" = "#E41A1C", "NON_LES" = "#377EB8")) +
  labs(title = "MOFA+ Factor 1 vs SA Abundance",
       x = "SA Abundance (%)",
       y = "Factor 1 Score",
       colour = "Lesional Status") +
  annotate("text", x = max(factors_df$sa_abundance, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.1,
           y = max(factors_df$Factor1, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.9,
           label = "rho = -0.776, p < 0.001",
           hjust = 0, size = 4.5) +
  theme_classic()

p_f1_sa

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/MOFA+/F1_by_SA_3view.png",
       plot = p_f1_sa, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```

## Step 5: **SA-Metabolite Associations**

-   Linear regression: metabolite \~ SA abundance (as SA abundance increases, do metabolite levels consistently increase or decrease?), adjusted for lesionality.

    -   metabolite = outcome, SA = predictor ("do samples with higher SA abundance have higher or lower levels of each metabolite, independent of whether the skin is lesional?”)

    -   Run separately for every metabolite to get a list of metabolites that significantly associate with SA abundance

    -   Adjusting for lesionality (covariate) – asks whether this relationship changes independent of lesionality (or if the changes are just a result of the lesional skin)

    -   Running hypothesis on all 38 metabolites seperately, each with a significance threshold of p\>0.05, means i could run into the multiple testing problem (false positives)

        -   So use Benjamin-Hochberg correction to control the false discover rate (FDR)

```{r}
#---------SA-Metabolite Linear Regression-------
results <- list()

for(metabolite in colnames(nmr_matched)) {
  df <- data.frame(
    metabolite = as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]),
    sa_abundance = master_table$sa_abundance[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)],
    lesional = master_table$lesional[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
  )
  model <- lm(metabolite ~ sa_abundance + lesional, data = df)
  results[[metabolite]] <- summary(model)$coefficients["sa_abundance", ]
}

# Combine results into a dataframe
results_df_sa <- do.call(rbind, results)
results_df_sa <- as.data.frame(results_df_sa)
results_df_sa$metabolite <- rownames(results_df_sa)

# Apply BH (FDR) correction
results_df_sa$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df_sa$`Pr(>|t|)`, method = "fdr")

# Rename columns
colnames(results_df_sa) <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value", "metabolite", "p_adjusted")

# Report nominally significant results (p < 0.05, uncorrected)
sig_nominal_sa <- results_df_sa[results_df_sa$p_value < 0.05, ]
sig_nominal_sa <- sig_nominal_sa[order(sig_nominal_sa$p_value), ]
sig_nominal_sa$metabolite <- NULL
print(sig_nominal_sa)

# ------ Visualise ---------
results_df_sa$significant <- results_df_sa$p_value < 0.05

sa_metabolites <- ggplot(results_df_sa, aes(x = Estimate, y = -log10(p_value), label = metabolite)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significant), size = 3) +
  geom_label_repel(data = subset(results_df_sa, p_value < 0.05),
                   aes(label = metabolite), size = 3,
                   box.padding = 0.5,
                   point.padding = 0.3,
                   max.overlaps = Inf) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate("text", x = max(results_df_sa$Estimate), y = -log10(0.05) + 0.1,
           label = "p = 0.05", color = "red", hjust = 1, size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey60", "steelblue"),
                     labels = c("p > 0.05", "p < 0.05"),
                     name = "") +
  labs(title = "S.Aureus-Metabolite Associations",
       x = "Estimate (Effect Size)",
       y = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/SA_metabolite_association.png",
       plot = sa_metabolites,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### 5.1. Also looking at Lesionality-Metabolite Correlation

adjusting for SA abundance

```{r}
#---------Lesionality-Metabolite Linear Regression-------
results <- list()

for(metabolite in colnames(nmr_matched)) {
  df <- data.frame(
    metabolite = as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]),
    sa_abundance = master_table$sa_abundance[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)],
    lesional = master_table$lesional[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
  )
  model <- lm(metabolite ~ lesional + sa_abundance, data = df)
  results[[metabolite]] <- summary(model)$coefficients["lesionalNON_LES", ]
}

# Combine results into a dataframe
results_df_les <- do.call(rbind, results)
results_df_les <- as.data.frame(results_df_les)
results_df_les$metabolite <- rownames(results_df_les)

# Apply BH (FDR) correction
results_df_les$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df_les$`Pr(>|t|)`, method = "fdr")

# Rename columns
colnames(results_df_les) <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value", "metabolite", "p_adjusted")

# Report nominally significant results (p < 0.05, uncorrected)
sig_nominal_les <- results_df_les[results_df_les$p_value < 0.05, ]
sig_nominal_les <- sig_nominal_les[order(sig_nominal_les$p_value), ]
sig_nominal_les$metabolite <- NULL
print(sig_nominal_les)
```

### 5.2. Also looked at TEWL-Metabolite Correlation

Adjusting for lesion status

```{r}
#---------TEWL-Metabolite Linear Regression-------
results <- list()
master_table$TEWL_mean <- as.numeric(master_table$TEWL_mean)

for(metabolite in colnames(nmr_matched)) {
  df <- data.frame(
    metabolite = as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]),
    lesional = master_table$lesional[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)],
    TEWL_mean = master_table$TEWL_mean[
      match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]
  )
  model <- lm(metabolite ~ TEWL_mean + lesional, data = df)
  results[[metabolite]] <- summary(model)$coefficients["TEWL_mean", ]
}

# Combine results into a dataframe
results_df_tw <- do.call(rbind, results)
results_df_tw <- as.data.frame(results_df_tw)
results_df_tw$metabolite <- rownames(results_df_tw)

# Apply BH (FDR) correction
results_df_tw$p_adjusted <- p.adjust(results_df_tw$`Pr(>|t|)`, method = "fdr")

# Rename columns
colnames(results_df_tw) <- c("Estimate", "Std_Error", "t_value", "p_value", "metabolite", "p_adjusted")

# Report nominally significant results (p < 0.05, uncorrected)
# Note: FDR correction too stringent for n=16 vs 38 metabolites
sig_nominal_tw <- results_df_tw[results_df_tw$p_value < 0.05, ]
sig_nominal_tw <- sig_nominal_tw[order(sig_nominal_tw$p_value), ]
sig_nominal_tw$metabolite <- NULL
print(sig_nominal_tw)

# ------ Visualise ---------
results_df_tw$significant <- results_df_tw$p_value < 0.05

TEWL_metabolites <- ggplot(results_df_tw, aes(x = Estimate, y = -log10(p_value), label = metabolite)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = significant), size = 3) +
  geom_label_repel(data = subset(results_df_tw, p_value < 0.05),
                   aes(label = metabolite), size = 3,
                   box.padding = 0.5,
                   point.padding = 0.3,
                   max.overlaps = Inf) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  annotate("text", x = max(results_df_tw$Estimate), y = -log10(0.05) + 0.1,
           label = "p = 0.05", color = "red", hjust = 1, size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("grey60", "steelblue"),
                     labels = c("p > 0.05", "p < 0.05"),
                     name = "") +
  labs(title = "TEWL-Metabolite Associations",
       x = "Estimate (Effect Size)",
       y = "-log10(p-value)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

ggsave("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/TEWL_metabolite_association.png",
       plot = TEWL_metabolites,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### 5.3. Spearman Correlation

For the SA-metabolite association

```{r}
# Align sa_abundance to nmr_matched row order once
sa_matched <- master_table$sa_abundance[
  match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]

# Run Spearman correlation for each metabolite
spearman_results <- lapply(colnames(nmr_matched), function(metabolite) {
  test <- suppressWarnings(
    cor.test(sa_matched, 
             as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]), 
             method = "spearman")
  )
  data.frame(
    metabolite = metabolite,
    rho = round(test$estimate, 3),
    p_value = round(test$p.value, 3)
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

rownames(spearman_results) <- NULL
spearman_results <- spearman_results[order(spearman_results$p_value), ]
print(spearman_results[spearman_results$p_value < 0.05, ])
```

For the lesionality-metabolite association

(ran wilcoxon rank-sum test bc lesionality is binary not continuous)

```{r}
lesional_matched <- master_table$lesional[
  match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)]

wilcox_results <- lapply(colnames(nmr_matched), function(metabolite) {
  test <- wilcox.test(as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]) ~ lesional_matched)
  data.frame(
    metabolite = metabolite,
    p_value = round(test$p.value, 3)
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

rownames(wilcox_results) <- NULL
wilcox_results <- wilcox_results[order(wilcox_results$p_value), ]
print(wilcox_results[wilcox_results$p_value < 0.05, ])

results_df[results_df$metabolite %in% c("Valine", "Alanine", "Glutamic acid"), ]
```

For the TEWL_metabolite association

```{r}
tewl_matched <- as.numeric(master_table$TEWL_mean[
  match(rownames(nmr_matched), master_table$NMR_sample_id)])

spearman_tewl <- lapply(colnames(nmr_matched), function(metabolite) {
  test <- suppressWarnings(
    cor.test(tewl_matched,
             as.numeric(nmr_matched[, metabolite]),
             method = "spearman")
  )
  data.frame(
    metabolite = metabolite,
    rho = round(test$estimate, 3),
    p_value = round(test$p.value, 3)
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

rownames(spearman_tewl) <- NULL
spearman_tewl <- spearman_tewl[order(spearman_tewl$p_value), ]
print(spearman_tewl[spearman_tewl$p_value < 0.05, ])

results_df_tw[results_df_tw$metabolite == "Glycerol", ]
```

### 5.4. Visualise Correlations

```{r}
# Create a matrix of p-values for all three analyses
all_metabolites <- unique(c(rownames(sig_nominal_tw), 
                             rownames(sig_nominal_sa),
                             rownames(sig_nominal_les)))

pval_matrix <- data.frame(
  TEWL = results_df_tw$p_value[match(all_metabolites, results_df_tw$metabolite)],
  S.Aureus = results_df_sa$p_value[match(all_metabolites, results_df_sa$metabolite)],
  Lesional_Status = results_df_les$p_value[match(all_metabolites, results_df_les$metabolite)]
)
rownames(pval_matrix) <- all_metabolites

# Create significance star matrix
# Create significance star matrix
star_matrix <- matrix("", nrow = nrow(pval_matrix), ncol = ncol(pval_matrix))
star_matrix[pval_matrix < 0.05]  <- "*"
star_matrix[pval_matrix < 0.01]  <- "**"
star_matrix[pval_matrix < 0.001] <- "***"

breaks <- seq(0, max(-log10(pval_matrix), na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 50)

p <- pheatmap(-log10(pval_matrix), 
         cluster_rows = TRUE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("#f7f7f7", "orange", "darkred"))(50),
         border_color = "white",
         main = "Metabolite Associations (-log10 p-value)",
         angle_col = 45,
         fontsize_row = 10,
         fontsize_col = 11,
         na_col = "lightgrey",
         display_numbers = star_matrix,
         number_color = "black",
         fontsize_number = 10,
         breaks = breaks,
         legend_breaks = c(0, 1.3, 2, 3),
         legend_labels = c("0\n(not significant)", 
                           "1.3\n(p = 0.05)", 
                           "2\n(p = 0.01)", 
                           "3\n(p = 0.001)"))

# Save with caption
png("~/Desktop/Research_Project/Scripts/Integration/metabolite_heatmap.png",
    width = 2400, height = 2200, res = 300)
grid::grid.newpage()

# Add margin at bottom for caption
grid::pushViewport(grid::viewport(y = 0.53, height = 0.92, just = "centre"))
grid::grid.draw(p$gtable)
grid::popViewport()

grid::grid.text("Colour scale: -log10(p-value). Higher values (darker red) indicate stronger significance.\n* p<0.05   ** p<0.01   *** p<0.001",
                x = 0.5, y = 0.04,
                gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 8, col = "grey40"))
dev.off()
```

##
